info.name: OnChainRisk Paid API
info.description:
Full OnChainRisk API for Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans.

**Setup:**
1. Sign up at https://onchainrisk.io/register and upgrade to Pro or higher
2. Dashboard → API Keys → Create a paid API key
3. Set env var `paid_api_key` to your key
4. Open any folder below — the top ones (Account → Analysis → ... → Labels) are safe to explore; the bottom ones (⚠ Advanced, ⚠ Destructive) need extra caution.

**Authentication:** every request uses the same header: `Authorization: Bearer {{paid_api_key}}`. No JWT, no CAPTCHA, no separate flow for different endpoint groups.

**Scope:** mirrors https://api.onchainrisk.io/docs for everything an external integrator can call with a paid API key. Signup / login / password reset / 2FA are intentionally not in this collection — they run in the dashboard UI (Turnstile CAPTCHA, JWT session) and are not part of the programmatic integration surface.

**Folder conventions:**
- `Account` → `Labels` — safe day-to-day paid API onboarding.
- `⚠ Advanced` — billing operations, MEV scan trigger, settings resets. Not destructive, but requires intent.
- `⚠ Destructive` — every DELETE and account deletion. Running any of these removes data.

A CI coverage script (`validate:paid-coverage`) ensures no core endpoint drifts out of sync with the Swagger spec.

variable.description: Paid API key (Pro+). Create at https://onchainrisk.io/dashboard/api-keys
USDC on Ethereum — safe honeypot/security test subject.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/v1/check (sync path) or POST /api/v1/multichain/analyze (async). Source: response.data.reportId.
Auto-populated when POST /api/v1/check returns 202 (heavy address routed to async queue). Source: response.data.reportId in the 202 payload.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/watchlist. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/labels. Source: response.data.id.
Auto-populated from GET /api/investigations (first item). Or run POST /api/investigate first.
Auto-populated from GET /api/keys (first returned key id).
Auto-populated when you run POST /api/support/tickets. Source: response.data.id.
Reserved. Billing flows are dashboard-only — not exposed in this collection.

exec: pm.test('async path: returned status poll id', () => pm.expect(d.reportId).to.be.a('string'));
// Stable validation envelope: validation errors always 422 with
// Added 2026-04-24 after the Tron/network mismatch incident.

response.name: 422 ADDRESS_NETWORK_MISMATCH (Tron + network=eth)
422 UNSUPPORTED_NETWORK (network=Ethereum)
422 INVALID_ADDRESS_CHECKSUM (Tron bad Base58Check)
422 COULD_NOT_DETECT_NETWORK (unrecognisable address)

request.description: Lightweight polling for async scans returned via 202 from POST /api/v1/check. Lifecycle: queued → analyzing → complete | failed. Does NOT consume quota. Poll every ~5 seconds until status is complete or failed (typically 1–5 minutes for heavy addresses).

item.name: Labels
item.description: Custom address labels. Bulk import/export for compliance teams.

item.name: API Keys
item.description: All /api/keys endpoints accept the paid API key (empirically verified). Sandbox-tier free users create keys via dashboard only.

request.description: Creates a new paid API key (your plan determines the max allowed). The full key is returned once in response.data.key — store it securely.

item.name: ⚠ Advanced
item.description: Not destructive, but require intent: triggers non-free compute, changes settings state.

request.description: Triggers a manual MEV scan. Non-free compute — use sparingly; cron typically handles this automatically.

item.name: ⚠ Destructive
item.description: Every request here removes data or changes a resource irreversibly. Double-check the {{sample_*}} id variables before running — you might overwrite state you care about. Where possible, test against dummy records first.

request.description: Revokes the specified key. If you revoke the same key you're authenticating with, subsequent requests will 401.
Archives the investigation (soft removal from active list).
